title: Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar
tagline: Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Shankaracharya Temple or Jyeshteshwara Temple is a Hindu temple situated on top of the Zabarwan Range in Srinagar in the Kashmir Valley of the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir,
overview.description: Shankaracharya Temple or Jyeshteshwara Temple is a Hindu temple situated on top of the Zabarwan Range in Srinagar in the Kashmir Valley of the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is dedicated to Shiva. The temple is at a height of 1,000 feet (300 m) above the valley floor and overlooks the city of Srinagar. The temple is accessible via a road that emerges off Boulevard road near Gagribal.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sonwar Bagh
topPlaces.description: Sonwar Bagh, sometimes referred to as Sonwar, is a residential area in the Badami Bagh Cantonment region on the outskirts of Srinagar city, in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is gradually becoming more commercial, as many schools, hotels and restaurants have been established there.

topPlaces.name: Indira Nagar, Srinagar
topPlaces.description: Indira Nagar is a residential area in the city of Srinagar in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It used to be a lake before but now only a few hundred meters of the lake are left.

topPlaces.name: Gungbugh
topPlaces.description: Gungbugh, is a small region of Srinagar city in Kashmir, situated on its outskirts. This place has been named Gungbugh due to a canal "Doodh Ganga" which passes nearby.

topPlaces.name: Amar Singh Club Ground
topPlaces.description: Amar Singh Club Ground is ground in Srinagar, in Jammu and Kashmir, India. The ground is part of the Amar Singh Club which was in the year 1933.

topPlaces.name: Sher-e-Kashmir Stadium
topPlaces.description: Sher-e-Kashmir Stadium is a cricket stadium located in the Sonwar Bagh neighbourhood of Srinagar in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is home to the Jammu and Kashmir Cricket Association and the Jammu and Kashmir cricket team which represents the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir in the Ranji Trophy and other domestic tournaments in the country.

topPlaces.name: Badami Bagh
topPlaces.description: Badami Bagh or Badam Bagah is a cantonment town on the outskirts of Srinagar in Jammu and Kashmir, India. Established in the 1920s as military barracks on a site containing several ancient Hindu and Budhhist ruins near Pandrethan, the Badami Bagh Cantonment currently serves as the headquarters of the Indian Army's Chinar Corps, with many of the army's soldiers residing here.

topPlaces.name: St. Luke's Church, Srinagar
topPlaces.description: St. Luke's Church is located within the compound of the Chest Diseases Hospital at the southwest slope of Shankaracharya Hill in the Dalgate area of Srinagar in Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: All Saints Church, Srinagar
topPlaces.description: All Saints Church, also known as All Hallows Church, is a historical Protestant church built in the late 19th century in Srinagar that is part of the Church of North India, a United Protestant denomination of Christianity. All Saints Church falls within the Diocese of Amritsar and along with Holy Family Catholic Church, is one of the two Christian churches in the city.

items.note: Sonwar Bagh, sometimes referred to as Sonwar, is a residential area in the Badami Bagh Cantonment region on the outskirts of Srinagar city, in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is gradually becoming more commercial, as many schools, hotels and restaurants have been established there.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (12 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (122 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar?
faq.a: Stays in Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shankaracharya Temple, Srinagar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sonwar Bagh, Indira Nagar, Srinagar, Gungbugh, Amar Singh Club Ground, Sher-e-Kashmir Stadium.
